studies.title: Use of a smartphone application (Orthopy App) to improve preoperative and postoperative therapy in patients with ACL rupture compared to conventional standard of care treatment - a randomized, monocentric study.

No evidence suggests it is about a different product or a generic study.
matched_elements: ACL rupture clinical area matches product description

_classification.reason: The study explicitly mentions randomization and compares interventions, indicating an interventional design typical of an RCT.

studies.title:
Use of a smartphone application (Orthopy App) to improve pre- and
postoperative therapy in patients after anterior cruciate ligament rupture
(with or without meniscal damage) or meniscal damage alone compared
to conventional standard of care treatment - a randomized, controlled,
multicenter study

_verification.reason: The study title explicitly mentions 'Orthopy App,' which is a clear variation of 'Orthopy bei Knieverletzungen,' and the clinical area aligns with the described ICD-10 codes for the product. The study focuses on the specific application of the app in therapy for knee injuries, matching the product description.

_classification.reason: The study explicitly mentions randomization and a controlled design, which are key indicators of an RCT.
